rationale: Both correctly diagnose the (None, payload) vs (payload, payload) starvation bug fixed by PR #4792 and correctly note it's already folded into the 0.24 rebase commit a560ed18, matching the ground-truth thread. X goes further with concrete, consistent file:line citations across the scheduler, coordinator, and both model runners, explains the async_chunk:true code path difference via partition_payload_list, and gives a tiered action plan (upgrade/cherry-pick/workaround) plus a note addressing other details from the env dump. Y reaches the same correct conclusion but with thinner grounding (fewer specific citations, an unverified 'sample_tokens()' framing) and less actionable follow-through, making X the more complete and grounded answer.
